title: December 2025 City Walk Outcomes
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: February 11, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Cynthia Esparza, Director
Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
DEPT: Community Engagement & Services
SUBJECT: December 2025 City Walk Outcomes
The City Walks Program is designed to enhance quality of life for residents while accelerating service delivery to vulnerable populations. The City Walks Program are data-driven and strategically aligned with broader organizational initiatives.
Coordinated by Clean and Safe, through the City Walks Program staff proactively addresses emerging Reno Direct service request trends, partners with community members to address concerns, and coordinates with the Reno Police Department's DEEDS (Directed Engagement and Enforcement Deployment Strategies) zones as a mechanism to connect residents, services, and enforcement efforts. Via the City Walks Program's integrated and interdepartmental approach, City Walks promote proactive problem-solving and timely interventions in the areas of need.
Walks occur four days per week. Monday and Wednesday walks are coordinated based on service request data citywide, while Tuesday and Thursday walks focus on the Business Improvement District (BID) in coordination with the Downtown Reno Partnership.
City Walks Departments
• Licensing and Code Enforcement
• Community Engagement and Services – Clean and Safe
• Parks and Recreation – Park and River Rangers, based on location
• Reno Police – Community Action & Outreach (CAO), Mobile Engagement Team (MET), and Parking Enforcement
The following is an overview of City Walk operations and outputs for December 2025.

Monday, December 1
• Locations: 8th Street Park, Record Street, and 7th Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 2 nuisance vehicles were issued 24-hour notice postings
o 1 request was submitted to Nevada Department of Transportation (NDOT) for unsecured fencing

Tuesday, December 2, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Holcomb Avenue, Ryland Street, Pine Street, and River Rock Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:

▪ 2 Broken windows
▪ 1 unsecured fencing

fields.authors: Cynthia Esparza, Director; Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
fields.tldr: This memo reports on City Walk operations conducted throughout December 2025, covering 16 walk dates across various Reno neighborhoods and the Downtown Business Improvement District. Participating departments included Clean and Safe, Code Enforcement, Reno Police Department, and Parks and Recreation. Outcomes included individual outreach contacts, nuisance vehicle notices, code enforcement cases, citations, and trash removal. The program coordinates proactively with Reno Direct service request data and the RPD DEEDS zones to connect residents with services and enforcement.
fields.key_stats: 16 walk dates in December 2025; 1 individual accessed REMSA services; approximately 75+ individuals contacted across all walks (majority declined services); 12 nuisance vehicles issued 24-hour notice postings; 18 code enforcement cases created (broken windows, debris, graffiti, unsecured fencing, outdoor storage, trespassing, lighting, vegetation); 11 citations issued for sitting/lying/sleeping/camping in public places; 14 yards of trash removed; 1 NDOT request submitted for unsecured fencing
fields.action_items: 1 request submitted to Nevada Department of Transportation (NDOT) for unsecured fencing (December 1 walk); ongoing coordination with Downtown Reno Partnership on Tuesday and Thursday BID walks; continued interdepartmental coordination with RPD DEEDS zones.
